name: AI Phishing Detector — ATT&CK Coverage
description: MITRE ATT&CK Enterprise techniques detected or referenced by the AI Phishing Detector portfolio project. Open in https://mitre-attack.github.io/attack-navigator/ to visualise coverage.

techniques.comment: Parent technique — generic phishing detection. Assigned when verdict=PHISHING and no URL IOC is present (e.g., BEC, executive impersonation).
Spearphishing Attachment — referenced in coverage but not directly detected (detector is text-only; no file attachment analysis). Known limitation documented in MODEL_CARD.md.
Spearphishing Link — primary detected sub-technique. Assigned when verdict=PHISHING and LLM IOC list contains a URL (http/https). Covers credential-harvesting links, fake login pages, open redirects.
Spearphishing via Service — referenced in threat model. Not directly detected (requires social media / third-party service context outside email).
Phishing for Information — ML model detects recon-style phishing (credential solicitation, low-payload messages designed to harvest info rather than deliver malware).
Spearphishing Link (Reconnaissance variant) — credential harvest patterns detected via TF-IDF lexicon: urgency + credential-solicitation token co-occurrence.
Masquerading — detected via header analysis: Reply-To domain mismatch, From display-name vs From domain mismatch (reply_to_mismatch flag).
Impersonation — LLM layer detects executive impersonation, brand impersonation (Microsoft, PayPal, USPS) from email content. ML detects via authority-impersonation lexicon in TF-IDF features.
User Execution — referenced as the downstream action after phishing delivery. The detector flags the delivery (T1566.002); T1204 represents what happens after the user clicks.
User Execution: Malicious Link — downstream execution phase for T1566.002 detections.
